feature.name: Account Settings
feature.description: Orchestrates account identity display, history navigation, notification and visibility preferences, blocked-account recovery and mutation, legal/support links, account deletion, and sign out.

actionScope.included: Profile and blocked-account recovery; review/payment history and host-app navigation; bounded preference changes; unblock, delete-account, and sign-out mutations; and configured privacy, support, and terms links.
excluded: Phone, email, version, visibility-copy, blocked-count, profile formatting, and blocked-profile enrichment are display projections rather than user actions.
Developer-only policy, Event Success, and manual-QA lab routes are configuration-gated diagnostics rather than Account Settings product actions.

Independent mutation guards allow unrelated navigation, external links, and destructive actions to overlap under DP-SETTINGS-ACCOUNT-004; external link results remain unobserved under DP-SETTINGS-ACCOUNT-005.

actions.description: Closes Account Settings through its canonical top-bar back control.
Invalidates the current profile provider from the inline account-status recovery surface.
Opens the current member's Review History.
Opens the current member's Payment History.
Requests the configured external Catch Host handoff.
Optimistically changes one of the seven supported notification or map-visibility preferences and rolls back on failure.
Invalidates the blocked-account provider after a list or profile-enrichment failure.
Removes one selected account from the current member's block list.
Requests the configured privacy-policy URL when present.
Opens the destructive confirmation and requests deletion after explicit confirmation.
Requests the configured help and support URL when present.
Requests the configured terms URL when present.
Signs out the current account through the shared auth-session mutation.

evidenceExceptions.reason: These Account Settings states have deterministic capture and Widgetbook evidence but no focused state-level test recorded by the authority.
History navigation and host handoff are focused-test and Widgetbook proven but do not have dedicated standalone state captures.
